terms.definition: A wiki page or document within a Nuclino workspace. Items contain a title and Markdown body content, and are the primary unit of knowledge in Nuclino.
A folder or group that organizes items hierarchically within a workspace. Collections can contain items and other collections. Collections may also carry Markdown content.
A named container belonging to a team that holds a hierarchy of items and collections. Workspaces can have custom fields that apply to all items within them.
The top-level organizational unit in Nuclino. A team owns one or more workspaces and manages member access.
A Nuclino account holder. Each API key is associated with a user and inherits that user's permissions.
A custom metadata attribute defined on a workspace that can be applied to items. Fields have a name and a type (e.g. text, number, date, select).
A binary attachment uploaded to and stored within Nuclino. Files are associated with a specific item and can be retrieved via a temporary download URL.
The body text of an item or collection, authored and stored in Markdown format. The content field is only returned when retrieving a single item, not in list responses.
An ordered array of UUIDs referencing the direct children (items or collections) of a collection or workspace. Represents the hierarchical structure of content.
A snippet of text from an item's content that shows the context of a full-text search match. Only present on items returned from a search request.
The pagination model used by the Nuclino API list endpoints. Use the `after` query parameter with the UUID of the last item in a page to retrieve the next page.
A secret credential passed in the Authorization HTTP header to authenticate API requests. Each key carries the permissions of its associated user account.
The maximum number of API requests allowed per API key per minute. Nuclino allows up to 150 requests per minute. Exceeding this limit returns HTTP 429.
The soft-delete destination for items and collections removed via the DELETE endpoint. Deleted items are moved to the trash rather than immediately destroyed.
Model Context Protocol — an integration standard that allows AI assistants to connect to Nuclino and read/write content programmatically. Nuclino provides a native MCP server.
